principles: Motion serves comprehension, never decoration; if an animation does not explain a change it is removed.
Favour opacity and small positional shifts over scale, rotation, or bounce.
Durations stay short so dense, data-heavy screens never feel sluggish under repeated use.
Every motion has an instant, non-animated equivalent for reduced-motion and low-power contexts.
Consistency over delight: the same change always animates the same way across the product.
entrance: Fade in over fast (100ms) with at most a 4px upward translate using the decelerate curve. No scale or overshoot.
exit: Fade out over fast (100ms) with the accelerate curve; element leaves without movement to avoid drawing attention away from the next focus target.
spatial_navigation: Cross-view transitions are an immediate content swap with a base (160ms) crossfade; no slide between routes so back/forward never feels directional or disorienting.
list_changes: Inserted and removed rows fade over fast (100ms); neighbours reflow with a base (160ms) position tween. Reordering is instant when more than ~20 rows move to avoid distracting churn.
status_change: Status pills transition colour and label over fast (100ms) and are always paired with a text/icon change so the meaning is never colour-or-motion only.
loading: Skeletons appear only after a 200ms delay to avoid flashes on fast responses; spinners use a steady, non-pulsing rotation and never bounce.
errors: Error surfaces appear with a fade (no shake); the field/region border and an inline message change together so the error is conveyed statically.
decorative_motion: Not used. No ambient, looping, or hover-triggered ornamental animation in work areas.
reduced_motion: All transitions collapse to duration 0 (instant state swap). Skeletons and final states render immediately; opacity may still cut between values but nothing translates, scales, or loops.
